---
title: "R Notebook"
output: html_notebook
---

```{r}

library(tidyverse)
library(tidyr)




fn <- read.csv("~/code/amino_optimize/USDA Data/food_nutrient.csv") |> select(fdc_id,nutrient_id, amount) 
nutrients <- read.csv("~/code/amino_optimize/USDA Data/nutrient.csv") |> select(id, name, unit_name)
names <- read.csv("~/code/amino_optimize/USDA Data/food_update_log_entry.csv") |> select(id, description)


fn1 <- fn |> pivot_wider( id_cols = fdc_id,
    names_from = nutrient_id,
    values_from = amount)


```


```{r}

nutrients$unit_name <- tolower(nutrients$unit_name)


# Create a named vector to map nutrient IDs to readable names
nutrient_lookup <- setNames(
  paste0(nutrients$name, " (", nutrients$unit_name, ")"),
  as.character(nutrients$id)
)

# Rename the nutrient columns (all columns except fdc_id)
fn2 <- fn1 |> 
  rename_with(
    ~ nutrient_lookup[.x],
    .cols = -fdc_id
  )


# Add food descriptions
fn_final <- fn2 |> 
  left_join(names, by = c("fdc_id" = "id")) |>
  select(fdc_id, description, everything())  # Put description near the front





```

```{r}

fnf <- fn_final |>
  filter(!is.na(`Cholesterol (mg)`) & `Cholesterol (mg)` == 0,
         !is.na(`Lactose (g)`) & `Lactose (g)` == 0,
         !is.na(`Protein (g)`) & `Protein (g)` > 2,
         !is.na(`Lysine (g)`) & `Lysine (g)` > 0,
         !is.na(`Leucine (g)`) & `Leucine (g)` > 0
         ) 

# Sort rows alphabetically by description
fnf <- fnf[order(fnf$description), ]

# Sort columns alphabetically while keeping fdc_id and description first
# Get all column names except fdc_id and description
other_cols <- setdiff(names(fnf), c("fdc_id", "description", "Protein (g)"))

# Sort the other columns alphabetically
other_cols_sorted <- sort(other_cols)

# Reorder columns: fdc_id, description, then alphabetically sorted others
fnf <- fnf[, c("fdc_id", "description", "Protein (g)", other_cols_sorted)]


```



```{r}

fnf_test <- fnf |> filter( str_detect(description, regex("[A-Z][A-Z]"))  == TRUE)

```



```{r}
# Curated subset of fnf: plain, consumer-relevant foods in dry AND cooked forms.
# Excludes: brands, restaurant items, canned/salted variants, junk food, spices,
# baby food, instant/precooked, enriched flour, condiments.

curated_descriptions <- c(
  # ── Grains ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  "Amaranth grain, uncooked",
  "Cereals, oats, regular and quick, not fortified, dry",
  "Cereals, oats, regular and quick, unenriched, cooked with water (includes boiling and microwaving), without salt",
  "Cornmeal, degermed, unenriched, yellow",
  "Pasta, dry, unenriched",
  "Pasta, cooked, enriched, without added salt",
  "Pasta, whole-wheat, cooked (Includes foods for USDA's Food Distribution Program)",
  "Rice, brown, long-grain, raw (Includes foods for USDA's Food Distribution Program)",
  "Rice, brown, long-grain, cooked (Includes foods for USDA's Food Distribution Program)",
  "Rice, white, long-grain, regular, raw, enriched",
  "Rice, white, long-grain, parboiled, enriched, cooked",   # best proxy for plain cooked white rice in this dataset
  "Rye grain",
  "Spelt, uncooked",
  "Wheat, KAMUT khorasan, uncooked",

  # ── Legumes ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  "Beans, pinto, mature seeds, raw (Includes foods for USDA's Food Distribution Program)",
  "Beans, pinto, mature seeds, cooked, boiled, without salt",
  "Lentils, raw",
  "Peas, green, split, mature seeds, raw",
  "Edamame, frozen, prepared",

  # ── Nuts ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  "Nuts, almonds",
  "Nuts, brazilnuts, dried, unblanched",
  "Nuts, cashew nuts, raw",
  "Nuts, hazelnuts or filberts",
  "Nuts, macadamia nuts, raw",
  "Nuts, pecans",
  "Nuts, pine nuts, dried",
  "Nuts, pistachio nuts, raw",
  "Nuts, walnuts, english",

  # ── Seeds & spreads ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  "Seeds, flaxseed",
  "Seeds, pumpkin and squash seed kernels, dried",
  "Seeds, sesame seed kernels, dried (decorticated)",
  "Seeds, sunflower seed kernels, oil roasted, without salt",
  "Peanut butter, smooth style, without salt",

  # ── Vegetables ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  "Asparagus, raw",
  "Broccoli, cooked, boiled, drained, without salt",
  "Corn, sweet, yellow, raw",
  "Corn, sweet, yellow, cooked, boiled, drained, without salt",
  "Kale, raw",
  "Kale, cooked, boiled, drained, without salt",
  "Mushrooms, oyster, raw",
  "Mushrooms, portabella, raw",
  "Mushrooms, shiitake, stir-fried",
  "Mushrooms, white, raw",
  "Peas, green, raw",
  "Peas, green, frozen, cooked, boiled, drained, without salt",
  "Potatoes, flesh and skin, raw",
  "Potatoes, baked, flesh and skin, without salt",
  "Spinach, raw",
  "Spinach, frozen, chopped or leaf, cooked, boiled, drained, without salt",
  "Sweet potato, cooked, baked in skin, flesh, without salt",

  # ── Other ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  "Miso"
)

fnf_curated <- fnf |>
  filter(description %in% curated_descriptions) |>
  arrange(description)

cat("Curated rows:", nrow(fnf_curated), "\n")
View(fnf_curated)

```






```{r}
# Find which nutrients are present vs absent in your data

# Get nutrient IDs that appear in your pivoted data (exclude fdc_id column)
nutrients_in_data <- colnames(fn1)[colnames(fn1) != "fdc_id"]

# Get all nutrient IDs from the nutrient reference file
all_nutrients <- as.character(nutrients$id)

# Find nutrients that are in the reference file but NOT in your data
missing_nutrients <- setdiff(all_nutrients, nutrients_in_data)

# Create a dataframe of missing nutrients with their details
missing_nutrient_info <- nutrients |> 
  filter(id %in% missing_nutrients) |>
  arrange(name)

# View the results
print(paste("Nutrients in your data:", length(nutrients_in_data)))
print(paste("Total nutrients in reference:", length(all_nutrients)))
print(paste("Missing nutrients:", length(missing_nutrients)))

# View the missing nutrients
View(missing_nutrient_info)
```
